completed.key_finding: True P0 advantage = ~3.3% (Standard 15x15). Tiebreak bug was sole source of 80-100% bias. Post-fix P0 balance achieved. No further mitigation needed.

DQN weights CORRUPTED (NaN in W2, b2, W3, b3 layers). Forward pass produces NaN Q-values; argmax defaults to index 0 => behavior is effectively random. Reported 92% winrate against Aggressive is invalid. Fresh (untrained) DQN control scored 0%.
completed.decision: Retrain DQN with gradient clipping + NaN detection + lower LR (0.0001) + Adam-style optimizer, OR abandon DQN paradigm for this version.

completed.key_finding: Combat randomness (±3) reduces Greedy mirror tiebreak rate by 7.4pp (88.6% -> 81.2%), increases conquest 7.4pp (11.4% -> 18.8%). Does NOT help underdogs (Greedy still ~80% vs Random). P0 balance unchanged. Recommendation: enable RANDOM_COMBAT as default.

running.detail: full-matrix-7x7/ directory exists but is empty (no JSON files present at check time)

needs_main_agent: DQN weights are NaN — retrain with gradient clipping + NaN detection + lower LR, or mark DQN as known-failed paradigm and move on?

verification_notes.data_file_anomalies: None detected. All summary.json values sane. No Inf/NaN in any results files except the DQN weights themselves (which the report correctly flags).
verification_notes.duplicates_with_earlier_experiments: P0 calibration pre-fix numbers (84.5%, 98.5%, 100%) match v0.4.0 results — correctly referenced as baseline. No new duplicates found.
